code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    Predicates are turned into readable phrases; triples that share the
    same subject are merged into one sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    import re

    # Mapping of predicates to natural language phrases.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the object value.
    phrase_map = {
        "alternativeName": "also known as {obj}",
        "bodyStyle": "features a {obj} body style",
        "engine": "is powered by a {obj} engine",
        "manufacturer": "was manufactured by {obj}",
        "relatedMeanOfTransportation": "is related to the {obj}",
        "transmission": "equipped with a {obj} transmission",
        "wheelbase": "has a wheelbase of {obj} inches",
        "builder": "was constructed by {obj}",
        "completionDate": "was completed on {obj}",
        "length": "measures {obj} millimetres in length",
        "powerType": "uses {obj} as its power source",
        "shipClass": "is classified as a {obj}",
        "shipDisplacement": "has a displacement of {obj} tonnes",
        "shipLaunch": "was launched on {obj}",
        "shipOrdered": "was ordered on {obj}",
        "shipPower": "is powered by {obj}",
        "topSpeed": "reaches a top speed of {obj} knots",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "christeningDate": "was christened on {obj}",
        "maidenVoyage": "embarked on its maiden voyage on {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "shipBeam": "has a beam width of {obj} metres",
        "shipInService": "has been in service since {obj}",
        "status": "is currently {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartDate": "became active on {obj}",
        "shipLaidDown": "was laid down on {obj}",
        "buildDate": "was built from {obj}",
        "cylinderCount": "features {obj} cylinders",
        "totalProduction": "total production reached {obj} units",
        "countryOrigin": "originated from {obj}",
        "diameter": "has a diameter of {obj} metres",
        "failedLaunches": "experienced {obj} failed launch(es)",
        "rocketStages": "consists of {obj} rocket stage(s)",
        "totalLaunches": "completed {obj} total launch(es)",
        "assembly": "was assembled in {obj}",
        "class": "belongs to the {obj} class",
        "designer": "was designed by {obj}",
        "modelYears": "was produced during {obj}",
        "country": "is from {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {obj}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {obj}",
        "designCompany": "was designed by {obj}",
        "productionStartYear": "production began in {obj}",
        "width": "measures {obj} metres in width",
        "layout": "utilizes a {obj} layout",
        "parentCompany": "is a subsidiary of {obj}",
        "operator": "is operated by {obj}",
        "product": "produces {obj}",
        "city": "is based in {obj}",
        "successor": "was succeeded by {obj}",
        "fate": "was ultimately {obj}",
        "keyPerson": "was led by {obj}",
        "subsidiary": "owns {obj} as a subsidiary",
        "comparable": "is comparable to {obj}",
        "finalFlight": "completed its final flight on {obj}",
        "function": "serves the function of {obj}",
        "launchSite": "launched from {obj}",
        "maidenFlight": "completed its maiden flight on {obj}",
        "capital": "capital is {obj}",
        "demonym": "its people are called {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "partialFailures": "had {obj} partial failure(s)",
        "site": "is located at {obj}",
        "headquarter": "headquartered in {obj}",
        "associatedRocket": "is associated with {obj}",
        "saint": "patron saint is {obj}",
        "employer": "is employed by {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "includes {obj} as an ethnic group",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {obj}",
        "anthem": "anthem is \"{obj}\"",
        "productionEndYear": "production ended in {obj}",
        "division": "includes {obj} as a division",
        "shipDraft": "has a draft of {obj}",
        "modelStartYear": "model year began in {obj}",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj} km²",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "extinctionDate": "became extinct on {obj}",
    }

    def predicate_phrase(p: str, obj: str) -> str:
        # Use explicit mapping if available
        if p in phrase_map:
            return phrase_map[p].format(obj=obj)
        # Fallback: split camelCase / PascalCase and prepend a generic verb
        words = re.sub(r'([a-z])([A-Z])', r'\1 \2', p).lower()
        return f"has {words} {obj}"

    # Helper to clean object strings (remove parentheses, extra quotes and surrounding whitespace)
    def clean_obj(o) -> str:
        """
        Clean the object value for textual rendering.
        Handles non‑string objects (e.g., numbers) by converting them to
        strings before applying the usual cleaning steps.
        """
        if not isinstance(o, str):
            return str(o)
        o = o.strip()
        # Remove surrounding double quotes if present
        if o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"'):
            o = o[1:-1]
        # Remove content in parentheses (e.g., units) and trim again
        if '(' in o:
            o = o.split('(')[0].strip()
        # Remove trailing commas and periods that may remain after cleaning
        o = o.rstrip(',.')
        return o

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        # clean the object before storing
        cleaned = clean_obj(t.object)
        grouped[t.subject].append(Triple(t.subject, t.predicate, cleaned))

    # Build clauses for each subject
    clause_parts = []
    for subj, items in grouped.items():
        # Ensure deterministic ordering of predicates for consistent output
        items = sorted(items, key=lambda x: x.predicate)
        # Build predicate phrases for this subject
        preds = [predicate_phrase(t.predicate, t.object) for t in items]
        if len(preds) == 1:
            body = preds[0]
        elif len(preds) == 2:
            body = " and ".join(preds)
        else:
            body = ", ".join(preds[:-1]) + ", and " + preds[-1]
        clause_parts.append(f"{subj} {body}")

    # Join all subject clauses into a single sentence
    if not clause_parts:
        return ""
    if len(clause_parts) == 1:
        sentence = clause_parts[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
